data.text: AN OPEN-LABEL, MULTICENTER, NON-RANDOMIZED PHASE 2 STUDY OF ELRANATAMAB (PF-06863135) MONOTHERAPY IN PARTICIPANTS WITH MULTIPLE MYELOMA WHO ARE REFRACTORY TO AT LEAST ONE PROTEASOME INHIBITOR, ONE IMMUNOMODULATORY DRUG AND ONE ANTI-CD38 ANTIBODY

MAGNETISMM-3

data.name: Elranatamab Monotherapy
data.description: All participants receive Elranatamab via subcutaneous administration with a step-up priming regimen (12 mg on Cycle 1 Day 1, 32 mg on Cycle 1 Day 4) followed by a maintenance dose of 76 mg on Cycle 1 Day 8 and thereafter. The frequency of the 76 mg dose changes from weekly (QW) for at least 6 cycles to every two weeks (Q2W) from Cycle 7 Day 1 if response criteria are met.

data.name: Age and Sex
data.text: Male or female participants age ≥18 years.

data.name: Compliance with study procedures
data.text: Participants who are willing and able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.

data.name: Prior diagnosis of MM
data.text: Prior diagnosis of MM as defined according to IMWG criteria (Rajkumar et al, 2014).

data.name: Measurable disease
data.text:
Measurable disease based on IMWG criteria as defined by at least 1 of the following:
a. Serum M-protein >0.5 g/dL by SPEP
b. Urinary M-protein excretion >200 mg/24 hours by UPEP
c. Serum immunoglobulin FLC ≥10 mg/dL (≥100 mg/L) AND abnormal serum immunoglobulin kappa to lambda FLC ratio (<0.26 or >1.65)

data.name: Refractory to IMiD
data.text: Refractory to at least one IMiD (as defined in Note below).

data.name: Refractory to PI
data.text: Refractory to at least one PI (as defined in Note below).

data.name: Refractory to anti-CD38 antibody
data.text: Refractory to at least one anti-CD38 antibody (as defined in Note below).

data.name: Relapsed or refractory to last anti-MM regimen
data.text: Relapsed or refractory to last anti-MM regimen. Note: Refractory is defined as having disease progression while on therapy or within 60 days of last dose in any line, regardless of response.

data.name: Prior BCMA-directed therapy status
data.text:
Cohort A: Has not received prior BCMA-directed therapy.
Cohort B: Has received prior BCMA-directed ADC or BCMA-directed CAR T-cell therapy, either approved or investigational.

data.name: ECOG performance status
data.text: ECOG performance status ≤2.

data.name: LVEF
data.text: LVEF ≥40% as determined by a MUGA scan or ECHO.

data.name: Adequate hepatic function
data.text:
Adequate hepatic function characterized by the following:
a. Total bilirubin ≤2 x ULN (≤3 x ULN if documented Gilbert’s syndrome);
b. AST ≤2.5 x ULN; and
c. ALT ≤2.5 x ULN

data.name: Adequate renal function
data.text: Adequate renal function defined by an estimated creatinine clearance ≥30 mL/min (according to the Cockcroft Gault formula, by 24-hour urine collection for creatinine clearance, or according to local institutional standard method).

data.name: Adequate BM function
data.text:
Adequate BM function characterized by the following:
a. ANC ≥1.0 × 109/L (use of granulocyte-colony stimulating factors is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing);
b. Platelets ≥25 × 109/L (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing); and
c. Hemoglobin ≥8 g/dL (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing).

data.name: Resolved acute effects of prior therapy
data.text: Resolved acute effects of any prior therapy to baseline severity or CTCAE Grade ≤1.

data.name: Informed consent
data.text: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix 1, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICD and in this protocol.

data.text: Stem cell transplant within 12 weeks prior to enrollment or active GVHD.

data.name: Impaired cardiovascular function
Ongoing peripheral neuropathy
data.text: Ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy.

data.name: History of peripheral neuropathy with prior BCMA-directed therapy
data.text: History of any grade peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy with prior BCMA-directed therapy (Cohort B).

data.name: History of GBS or severe peripheral motor polyneuropathy
data.text: History of GBS or GBS variants, or history of any Grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy.

data.name: Active infections
data.text: Active HBV, HCV, SARS-CoV2, HIV, or any active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection. Active infections must be resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

data.name: Other active malignancy
data.text: Any other active malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment, except for adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer, or carcinoma in situ.

data.name: Other significant medical/psychiatric conditions or surgery
data.text: Other surgical (including major surgery within 14 days prior to enrollment), medical or psychiatric conditions including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator’s judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

data.name: Previous anti-BCMA bispecific antibody treatment
data.text: Previous treatment with an anti-BCMA bispecific antibody.

data.name: Previous investigational drug administration
data.text: Previous administration with an investigational drug within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of study intervention used in this study (whichever is longer).

data.name: Study staff or family members
data.text: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.

data.name: Hypersensitivity to study intervention
data.text: Known or suspected hypersensitivity to the study intervention or any of its excipients.

data.name: Live attenuated vaccine administration
data.text: Live attenuated vaccine must not be administered within 4 weeks of the first dose of study intervention.

data.name: Amendment 1
data.summary: 1. Throughout: Removed inclusion of participants age <18 years and all corresponding assent language (including removing reference to “legally authorized representative” in Section 10.1.3). 2. SoA, Section 8.1.1 and Appendix 10: FLC now specified as “serum FLC” for clarity. 3. SoA, Section 1.1, Section 4.3, Section 6.1.1 and Section 6.6.3: Removed investigator’s discretion with regard to the transitions between dosing intervals (QW, Q2W). 4. SoA and Section 8.2.4: Clarified timing of ECGs: removed “postdose” as there are no scheduled postdose collections, and removed the 60-minute predose window. 5. SoA: Added rows for “Disease response assessment (per IMWG criteria)” and “Assessment of BOR” 6. SoA: Added detail around timing of UPEP, UIFE and imaging. Additional detail added to align optional BMB sampling with optional BMA sampling. 7. SoA and Section 8.2.7: Added “serum test required at screening” for clarity in alignment with footnote in Table 8 of Appendix 2. 8. SoA: Removed “within 2 hour” requirement for biomarker sample collection. 9. SoA: Added sBCMA samples beyond Cycle 7 to characterize the impact of Q2W switch on sBCMA levels from Cycle 7 and on. 10. Section 4.2.1: Modified to remove mention of participants in C1071001 who have previously received BCMA-directed BsAb. 11. Sections 4.2.2: Added MRD threshold of 10-5, with the potential to measure MRD frequency up to 10-6. 12. Section 4.4: Modified to specify that the study will be completed when all participants have been followed for OS for at least 2 years from the date of enrollment. 13. Section 5.1 (Inclusion criterion #15): Removed “except for AEs not constituting a safety risk for the participant by investigator judgment” 14. Section 5.2 (Exclusion criteria #2, #3 and #6): Modified for clarity. 15. Section 6.1: Corrected vial size from 2 mL to 6 mL. 16. Section 6.1.1: Corrected timing of PF-06863135 administration from Day 21 to Day 22. 17. Section 6.6.1: Updated redosing criteria for clarity. 18. Section 6.6.1 and Table 4: Removed “or is Grade 2 and is not considered by the investigator to be a safety risk to the participant” 19. Table 4 footnote b: Added “or permanent discontinuation.” 20. Section 8.1: Removed “assessment of best response, date of response onset, and date of progression (if applicable)” 21. Section 8.1.1: Minor edits for clarity 22. Section 8.1.5: Added “(as applicable)” to “Best response and date of disease progression…” 23. Section 8.2.2: Removed “Any abnormal physical examination findings observed prior to administration of the first dose of PF-06863135 should be recorded in the Medical History CRF.” 24. Section 8.3.1: Removed “for survival” from “During the long-term follow-up period in this study…” 25. Section 8.8.1: Added MRD threshold of 10-5. 26. Section 8.8.4 – Section 8.8.7: Removed sample volumes as these will be specified in the laboratory manual. Also added “(for predose samples, collection should occur prior to administration of PF-06863135 on that day)” to clarify that predose sample collections are to occur on the same day as treatment. 27. Section 9.1.2: Added rationale for hypotheses. 28. Section 9.2: Changed power from 80% to 90% to detect ORR of 48% (previously 45%) in Cohort A and 34% (previously 31%) in Cohort B. 29. Section 9.4.5: Added section on other safety analyses. 30. Section 9.5 (with references to Section 9.5 added to Section 2.3.1 and Section 8.3.8): Added interim safety assessment section for Grade ≥3 CRS, Grade ≥3 ICANS, Grade ≥4 non-hematologic events (excluding CRS and ICANS) and Grade 5 hematologic events. Also added “If the efficacy boundary is crossed, enrollment to the study will continue up to the specified number of participants, and all ongoing participants will continue with scheduled visits per the Schedule of Activities” to clarify that the study will not be stopped for efficacy at IA. 31. Appendix 2 (Table 8): Added LDH to lab assessments. Appendix 10: Removed “Participants will continue in the last confirmed response category until there is confirmation of progression or improvement to a higher response status.”

data.name: Amendment 5
data.summary: This amendment incorporates changes previously included in Amendment 2 (which was based on Protocol Amendment 1) and country-specific Amendments 3 and 4 (which were also based on Protocol Amendment 1); therefore Protocol Amendment 5 brings all the amendments into one document. Following is a summary of additional changes: 1. As per regulatory requirements (US FDA), updates to describe peripheral neuropathy (including GBS) as an important potential risk of elranatamab, and measures to mitigate risk including (a) addition of various new safety monitoring measures, (b) modification to participant selection (exclusion) for those potentially at higher risk; (c) addition of dose modification rules for peripheral neuropathy; (d) addition of recommended work-up for peripheral neuropathy; and (e) addition of considerations regarding concomitant medications. In addition, the criteria for placing the study on temporary hold (specifically related to neuropathy or other IR neurological AEs) were fully defined. 2. Additional information was added in Background (Section 2.2) based on Phase 1 results. 3. To mitigate CRS and ICANS, a 2 step-up priming dose approach (which includes premedication, and administration of elranatamab on C1D1 and C1D4 before the first full dose of elranatamab) was incorporated for the first week of study intervention. 4. An additional visit, hospitalization periods, safety assessments, sample collections, and PROs are included in the SoA and Section 6. 5. For participant selection, clarification was added around DVT and window for prior transplant. 6. In order to capture all potential AEs with elranatamab, including late onset immune-related neurologic AEs, the safety reporting period after last dose of study intervention has been increased to 90 days. 7. Contraception use has been extended from 28 days to 90 days after the last dose of study intervention (as required by France HA). 8. A Steering Committee, to be involved with general oversight of the study, was established. 9. Staging systems for multiple myeloma were more explicitly defined. 10. Interim analysis set populations (for both cohort A and B) were further defined. 11. Given the INN name is established, PF-06863135 has been replaced with the INN name, elranatamab. 12. Added Appendix 12: Japan Regulatory Requirements. 13. Clarifications were made throughout, including the SoA.

data.name: Amendment 6
data.summary: 1. Revised enrollment hold rules per regulatory requirement (US FDA): the posterior probability threshold of ≥80% is used for stopping rules for safety for treatment-related Grade 3-4 GBS (including variants), and treatment-related Peripheral Neuropathy/IR Neurologic AEs (section 9.5.2 and Appendix 13). 2. Exclusion criteria for ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy was revised (per regulatory requirements, US FDA). 3. Dose modifications for peripheral neuropathy were revised (per regulatory requirements, US FDA). 4. Clarification added for dose delays/interruptions, including windows (SoA, section 6.1.1, section 6.6.2). 5. Clarified imaging schedule, including requirement that imaging be conducted every 12 weeks for participants with baseline extramedullary disease (SoA). 6. Clarification added for availability of study treatment after end of study (section 6.7). 7. Clarified requirements for laboratory assessments (section 8.1.1). 8. Removed requirement for investigator review of completed PRO responses (including EORTC QLQ CIPN20, as regular neurologic exams are performed by the investigator) (section 8.1.4, and reference in section 2.3.1). 9. Clarified requirements for exposure during pregnancy (section 8.3.5.1). 10. Clarifications were made throughout, including SoA and Appendices.

data.name: Amendment 7
data.summary: 1. The total number of planned participants was revised from 150 to 180 participants allowing for more robust datasets. Power/sample size calculations updated to be based on normal distribution from exact method (sections 1, 4.1, 9.2, 9.5). 2. Interim analyses (IA) revised to include a more robust data set (increased N from 60 to 90 for Cohort A) and added efficacy IA for Cohort B. IAs to be based on actual number with adequate follow-up at time of IA (section 9.5). 3. The follow-up period for final analysis was revised (section 9.2). 4. Added statement that interim analyses results can be used by the sponsor for decision-making and a statement about requirements before interim analysis can be conducted in accordance with the sponsor’s SOPs (sections 7, 9.5). 5. Clarification that permitted concomitant treatments include infection prophylaxis and use of IV immunoglobulins; added further considerations for oral contraceptives (section 6.5.2). 6. Clarification for dose modifications, including those for priming doses (sections 6.6.1, 6.6.2, Table 4). 7. Revised local laboratory collections to include serum quantitative immunoglobulins and plasma cells (SoA, Appendix 2). 8. Revised BMA sampling times; clarified BMA sampling times in cases of CR by all other parameters except bone marrow plasma cells (SoA). 9. Made BMB sampling optional to better align with global clinical practice (SoA, section 8.1.2, 8.8.3). 10. Added immunofluorescence and flow cytometry for the determination of sCR to better align with global clinical practice (section 8.1.2, Appendix 10). 11. Clarified allowed sample types for MRD evaluation (SoA, section 8.8.1). 12. Added allowance for use of sample in the event that the index myeloma clone is not identified from screening sample (section 8.8.1) 13. Reduced frequency of administration of PROs after Year 1 (SoA). 14. Clarified imaging requirements after EMD resolution/disappearance (SoA). 15. Removed BOR assessment (SoA). 16. For discontinuation reasons, added lack of efficacy and completed (sections 7.1 and 7.2). 17. Added more detailed description for MRD analysis (section 9.4.3). 18. Clarifications were made throughout multiple sections, including section 1, 3-5, 8, SoA and Appendices.

data.name: Amendment 8
data.summary: 1. Added confirmed before PD (Section 1.1, 6.1.1, and 9.4.2) 2. Clarified that all participants will still be enrolled for the final analysis if the interim efficacy boundary is crossed and operating characteristics for the design are in the SAP (Section 9.5.1) 3. Updated the posterior probability threshold of ≥90% to ≥80% for Grade 3-4 CRS/ICANS and Grade 4 treatment related nonhematologic for the interim safety assessments (Section 9.5.2 and Table 8) 4. Editorial update (Appendix 10).

data.name: Amendment 9
data.summary: 1. Added key secondary endpoint, ORR by BICR baseline EMD status (cohort A) (Sections 1.1, 1.2, 3, 9.1.1, 9.4.3). 2. Added CMV testing (Section 1.3, Table 10). 3. Updated alternative BMA sample types (Sections 1.3, 8.8.1). 4. Updated elranatamab clinical data (C1071001 and C1071003 study results) (Section 2.2.3, 4.2.1). 5. Added infection as a potential risk of clinical significance for elranatamab and made updates to summary of data throughout (Section 2.3.1) 6. Added more detailed guidance for infection prophylaxis (Section 6.5.2, and a new appendix, Appendix 14). 7. Added guidance about COVID-19 testing and treatment (Section 6.5.2) 8. Updates to align with SAP, including Interim Analysis plans for Cohort B (Section 9) 9. Additions to Japan Regulatory Requirements (Section 10.12.1) 10. Editorial updates (Sections 4.3, 10.15, 11).

data.name: Amendment 10
data.summary: 1. Revised elranatamab contraception requirements (WOCBP: aligned with updated elranatamab PK data as described in IB; Men: aligned with calculated safety margin between seminal transfer and estimated MABEL). (Sections 1.3, 4.2.3, 5.3.1, 10.4). 2. Revised elranatamab clinical study summaries, referring protocol users to the IB for the most current information (Sections 2.2.3, 2.3.1). 3. Added more detailed descriptions in Sections 1.3 and 8.1.1 for when serum FLC should be used to assess disease response and about repeat sampling for confirmation of disease progression to match descriptions in Section 10.10. 4. Added definition of ‘Final Analysis Evaluable Set’ to align with statistical analysis plan (Section 9.3). 5. Revised descriptions throughout Section 10.1 to improve clarity/provide additional detail (including, but not limited to, added description of study data collection, processing and storage systems [Section 10.1.4]; clarification of protection of personally identifiable information [Section 10.1.5]; description of QTLs [Section 10.1.6]; clarifications for study start and closure [Section 10.1.8]; clarification of publication policy [Section10.1.9]; clarifications about contact card [Section 10.1.10]). 6. Removed requirement to notify study team when treatment is restarted following SARS-CoV-2 infection as protocol already includes relevant guidance (Section 10.8.4). 7. General editorial revision throughout to specify “confirmed” PD in descriptions of study intervention discontinuation and disease response requirements for consistency with descriptions in Sections 6.1.1 and 8.1. 8. General editorial revisions throughout to correct grammatical errors, to maintain consistency, and/or to improve clarity. 9. Sponsor legal address added (page 1). This amendment incorporates all revisions to date, including amendments made at the request of country health authorities and IRBs/ECs and any protocol administrative change letter(s).

raw.name: Elranatamab (PF-06863135) administration
raw.description: Two step-up priming doses of 12 mg on C1D1 then 32 mg on C1D4, followed by 76 mg starting on C1D8 QW. Minimum of 2 days should be maintained between the C1D1 and C1D4 doses, minimum of 3 days between C1D4 dose and the first full dose (C1D8); a minimum of 6 days should be maintained between doses thereafter. If participant has received QW dosing for at least 6 cycles and has achieved a PR or better persisting for ≥2 months, the dose interval will be changed from QW to Q2W (eg, beginning C7D1). See Section 6 (including Section 6.6 dose modifications).

raw.name: Hospitalization
raw.description: Hospitalization required for at least 2 days (~48 hr) for C1D1 and 1 day (~24 hr) for C1D4. Hospitalization from C1D1 to C1D5 may be considered. See Section 6.1.

raw.name: Disease response assessment (per IMWG criteria)
raw.description: To be conducted on a 28-day (±1 wk) interval whether dose given or not (ie, the 28-day interval (±1 wk) between assessments should be maintained regardless of dose delays/interruptions). Participants with measurable disease by SPEP or UPEP cannot have response assessed by serum FLC. Except for sCR, serum FLC levels should only be used for response assessment when both the serum and urine M component levels are deemed not measurable or uninterpretable. Serum FLC cannot replace UPEP/UIFE in any situation where urine M component is measurable at baseline. *For participants who discontinue study intervention without confirmed PD: perform at EOT visit, then at least Q4W (±1 wk) until confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to follow-up, death or defined end of study. See Section 8.1 for complete details (including requirements for repeat testing for confirmation of PD).

raw.name: SPEP, SIFE, serum FLC
raw.description: Screening and C1D1 (if not done within 72 hours prior to dosing). Then, to be conducted on a 28-day (±1 wk) interval whether dose given or not (ie, the 28-day interval (±1 wk) between assessments should be maintained regardless of dose delays/interruptions). *For participants who discontinue study intervention without confirmed PD: perform at EOT visit, then at least Q4W (±1 wk) until confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to follow-up, death or defined end of study. See Section 8.1.1 for complete details (including requirements for repeat testing for confirmation of PD).

raw.name: UPEP, UIFE (24-hour urine collection required)
raw.description: Screening and C1D1 (if not done within 72 hours prior to dosing). Then, to be conducted on a 28-day (±1 wk) interval whether dose given or not (ie, the 28-day interval (±1 wk) between assessments should be maintained regardless of dose delays/interruptions). *For participants who discontinue study intervention without confirmed PD: perform at EOT visit, then at least Q4W (±1 wk) until confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to follow-up, death or defined end of study. For participants without measurable disease in the urine at baseline, UPEP/UIFE is required (minimum) at suspected VGPR or CR/sCR, or at suspected PD. If an evaluable 24-hour urine collection is missed, another attempt for collection should be made within 7 days of the missed assessment. See Section 8.1.1 for complete details (including requirements for repeat testing for confirmation of PD).

raw.name: Imaging (PET/CT, CT or MRI)
raw.description: Time points for disease assessments described in notes At screening, at suspected CR/sCR (if not performed within the prior 6 weeks), at suspected PD from EMD, and annually if not done within past 12 months. In addition, for participants with EMD at screening, every 12 wks (± 1 wk), and also at suspected MR or PR. Once all EMD has resolved/disappeared, imaging can be conducted every 24 weeks (or earlier if clinically indicated) and at suspected PD. For participants with only skin involvement, skin lesions should be measured with a ruler every 4 wks (±1 wk). Perform until confirmed PD, WOC, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to FU, death, or defined EOS. See Section 8.1.3

raw.name: BMA
raw.description: Time points for disease assessments described in notes At screening, suspected CR and then after 6 months, 12 months, and yearly (± 1 month) after achieving CR (provided CR is maintained clinically) until WOC, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to FU, death, or defined EOS. Optional (investigator discretion) at suspected PD. If all criteria for CR are met except percent plasma cells in BM, then additional BMA should be performed every 3 months until CR is achieved, as long as other CR criteria are maintained. See Section 8.1.2

QW to Q2W Dosing Transition Criteria
data.description: Criteria for changing dose interval from weekly to every 2 weeks
data.text: Participant has received QW dosing for at least 6 cycles and has achieved an IMWG response category of PR or better persisting for at least 2 months

data.name: Redosing Criteria - Start of Cycle
data.description: All parameters must be met before re-treatment at the start of any new cycle
data.text: ANC ≥1.0 × 10^9/L; Platelet count ≥25 × 10^9/L; Recovery of treatment-related nonhematologic toxicities to baseline or Grade ≤1 severity; No ongoing CRS or ICANS (any grade); Recovery of treatment-emergent peripheral neuropathy to Grade <1 severity

data.name: Priming Dose Completion Requirement
data.description: Both priming doses must be received before full dose
data.text: Participants must receive both elranatamab priming doses (12 mg C1D1 and 32 mg C1D4) before receiving the planned full dose (76 mg)

data.name: Continued Treatment Criteria
data.description: Criteria for continuing study intervention
data.text: Each participant may receive study intervention until confirmed disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, withdrawal of consent, or study termination

data.name: Post-Discontinuation Disease Assessment Continuation
data.description: Conditions for continuing disease assessments after treatment discontinuation without confirmed PD
data.text: For participants who discontinue study intervention without confirmed PD: perform assessments at EOT visit, then at least Q4W (±1 wk) until confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to follow-up, death or defined end of study

data.name: Screening Lab Assessment Timing for C1D1
data.description: Screening labs may substitute for C1D1 if within 72 hours
data.text: SPEP, SIFE, serum FLC, UPEP, UIFE required at Screening and C1D1 (if not done within 72 hours prior to dosing)

data.name: CRS Susceptibility Exclusion
data.description: Exclusion of participants particularly susceptible to CRS complications
data.text: Participants with impaired cardiac function or clinically significant cardiovascular disease are excluded to ensure tolerance of potential CRS events

data.name: Peripheral Neuropathy Exclusion Criteria
data.description: Exclusion of participants susceptible to new or worsening peripheral neuropathy
data.text: Excludes participants with POEMS syndrome, history of GBS or GBS variants, ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy, and history of prior neuropathy with BCMA-directed drugs

data.name: Imaging at Suspected CR/sCR Condition
data.description: Imaging required at suspected CR/sCR unless recently performed
data.text: Imaging required at suspected CR/sCR if not performed within the prior 6 weeks

data.name: Urine Response Assessment Limitation
data.description: Conditions for when serum FLC can be used for response assessment
data.text: Participants with measurable disease by SPEP or UPEP cannot have response assessed by serum FLC. Serum FLC levels should only be used for response assessment when both the serum and urine M component levels are deemed not measurable or uninterpretable. Serum FLC cannot replace UPEP/UIFE in any situation where urine M component is measurable at baseline.

data.name: Screening to Treatment Transition
data.text: Participant must meet all eligibility criteria (inclusion and exclusion) during screening window (Day -28 to Day -1) and be registered with IRT

data.name: QW to Q2W Dose Interval Transition
data.text: If participant has received QW dosing for at least 6 cycles and has achieved a PR or better persisting for ≥2 months, the dose interval will be changed from QW to Q2W (eg, beginning C7D1)

data.name: Priming Dose Escalation C1D1 to C1D4
data.text: Dose of elranatamab should be increased from 12 mg (C1D1) to 32 mg on C1D4 as long as the participant meets the redosing criteria. Minimum of 2 days between C1D1 and C1D4.

data.name: Priming Dose Escalation C1D4 to Full Dose C1D8
data.text: Dose of elranatamab should be increased from 32 mg (C1D4) to 76 mg on C1D8 as long as the participant meets the redosing criteria. Minimum of 3 days between C1D4 and C1D8.

data.name: Dose Deferral for Priming Dose Not Meeting Criteria
data.text: If a participant does not meet redosing criteria on C1D4 and C1D8, dosing should be deferred until the criteria are met

data.name: C1D4 Dose Delay and Resumption
data.text: If the C1D4 dose (32 mg) cannot be administered within the protocol-defined window, elranatamab treatment may resume at the planned C1D4 dose (32 mg) upon meeting the re-treatment criteria. Upon re-treatment, if the C1D4 dose (32 mg) was tolerable, the elranatamab dose should be increased to the planned full dose (76 mg) approximately 4 days later.

data.name: QW to Q2W Dosing Decision
data.description: At or after Cycle 7 Day 1, assess whether participant has received QW dosing for at least 6 cycles and achieved PR or better persisting for ≥2 months to transition to Q2W dosing

data.name: Redosing Decision at Start of Each Cycle
data.description: At the start of each new cycle, verify all redosing criteria are met (ANC, platelets, nonhematologic toxicity recovery, no CRS/ICANS, peripheral neuropathy recovery) before administering next dose

data.name: Priming Dose Escalation Decision at C1D4
data.description: Decision to escalate from 12 mg priming dose to 32 mg priming dose on C1D4 based on redosing criteria

data.name: Full Dose Escalation Decision at C1D8
data.description: Decision to escalate from 32 mg priming dose to 76 mg full dose on C1D8 based on redosing criteria

data.name: Disease Response Assessment Decision
data.description: Every 28 days (±1 week), assess disease response per IMWG criteria to determine continuation, confirmation of PD, or response category

data.name: Post-Discontinuation Assessment Continuation Decision
data.description: For participants who discontinue without confirmed PD, decide whether to continue Q4W disease assessments based on confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, subsequent therapy initiation, lost to follow-up, death, or end of study

data.name: Imaging at Suspected CR/sCR Decision
data.description: At suspected CR/sCR, determine whether imaging is required based on whether imaging was performed within the prior 6 weeks

Collection 'beyond Cycle 7' and 'from Cycle 7 and on' implies repeated collection at scheduled intervals or visits after Cycle 7.

data.text: STUDY ASSESSMENTS AND PROCEDURES The investigator (or an appropriate delegate at the investigator site) must obtain a signed and dated ICD before performing any study-specific procedures. Study procedures and their timing are summarized in the SoA. Protocol waivers or exemptions are not allowed. Safety issues should be discussed with the sponsor immediately upon occurrence or awareness to determine whether the participant should continue or discontinue study intervention. Adherence to the study design requirements, including those specified in the SoA, is essential and required for study conduct. All screening evaluations must be completed and reviewed to confirm that potential participants meet all eligibility criteria. The investigator will maintain a screening log to CCI
